items.situation: student asking about exam format or study materials
items.speaker: What format is the midterm going to be?

options.A: I'll email everyone the study guide tonight.
options.B: The midterm is worth thirty percent.
options.C: Formatting the paper took me hours.
options.D: Which class did you say you missed?

items.explanation: 正确答案 A 没有直接回答考试是什么形式，而是解决学生背后的真正需求——提供复习指南，符合 context_shift 范式。B 是离题但语法正确的句子，谈论的是考试分值而非形式，答非所问。C 是语义联想陷阱，抓住 "format/formatting" 这个词去谈排版论文，与考试形式无关。D 是错误的问题类型，反问学生缺了哪节课，转移了话题，没有解决需求。

items.situation: student asking about a reading list for a course
items.speaker: Did the professor post the reading list yet?

options.A: I really enjoy reading by the window.
options.B: Yes, it went up this morning.
options.D: No, I haven't met her yet.

items.explanation: 正确答案 B 直接回答了 yes/no 问题，明确说阅读清单今早已发布，符合 direct_topical 范式。A 是语义联想陷阱，抓住 "reading" 这个词去谈个人阅读习惯，没有回答清单是否发布。C 也是语义陷阱，抓住 "professor" 去谈办公室位置，答非所问。D 是错误的问题类型，回答的是 "你见过教授吗" 而不是 "清单发了吗"。

items.situation: student asking about a campus event or concert
items.speaker: There's a student film screening this Friday.

options.A: The film was shot entirely on campus.
options.B: I returned the movie tickets already.
options.C: Want to grab seats together beforehand?
options.D: The projector broke down last week.

items.explanation: 正确答案 C 用一个反问推进对话，提议一起提前占座，自然回应了对方分享的活动信息，符合 counter_question 范式。A 是语义联想陷阱，抓住 "film" 去谈拍摄地点，没有推进对话。B 是离题但语法正确的句子，谈论退电影票，与本次放映无关。D 也是语义陷阱，抓住放映相关的 "projector" 去谈上周设备故障，偏离了对方的邀约意图。

items.speaker: Why are you closing so early today?

options.A: The store is two blocks down.
options.B: We open again at nine tomorrow.
options.C: Closets are on sale this week.

items.explanation: 正确答案 D 用自然的会话套语 "I'm afraid" 委婉解释提前关门的原因是人手不足，直接回应了 "为什么"，符合 idiomatic 范式。A 是错误的问题类型，回答的是地点（在哪）而不是原因（为什么）。B 是离题但语法正确的句子，谈论的是次日营业时间，没回答今天为何早关。C 是语义联想陷阱，把 "closing" 谐音联想到 "closets" 去谈打折，完全误读。

items.situation: expressing concern about a friend's situation
items.speaker: You've seemed pretty down since the move.

options.A: Want to talk about it over coffee?
options.B: The movers were late that day.
options.C: I moved the boxes upstairs already.
options.D: Downtown is just past the bridge.

items.explanation: 正确答案 A 用一个温暖的反问推进对话，提议一起喝咖啡聊聊，自然回应了对方的关心，符合 counter_question 范式。B 是语义联想陷阱，抓住 "move" 去谈搬家公司迟到，没有回应情绪关心。C 也是语义陷阱，抓住 "move/moved" 去谈搬箱子，答非所问。D 是错误的问题类型/语义陷阱，把 "down" 字面理解成方位去谈市中心位置，完全误读了对方的关心。

items.situation: friend inviting another to a study group or social event
items.speaker: Where is the study group meeting tonight?

options.A: I studied there all last semester.
options.D: Because the exam is next week.

items.explanation: 正确答案 B 直接回答了 "where"（哪里），给出具体地点——图书馆三楼，符合 direct_topical 范式。A 是语义联想陷阱，抓住 "study" 去谈自己上学期的学习经历，没回答地点。C 是错误的问题类型，回答的是 "when"（几点）而不是 "where"。D 也是错误的问题类型，回答的是 "why"（为什么）而不是地点。

items.situation: student asking about dorm maintenance issue
items.speaker: How do I get the leaky faucet fixed?

options.A: The water tastes a bit strange lately.
options.B: Plumbing is such an old profession.
options.C: Have you filed a maintenance request yet?
options.D: Yes, the sink is in the bathroom.

items.explanation: 正确答案 C 用一个反问推进对话，引导学生先提交维修申请，是解决问题的实际下一步，符合 counter_question 范式。A 是语义联想陷阱，抓住 "faucet" 关联到自来水去谈水味，没回答如何修理。B 也是语义陷阱，抓住漏水关联的 "plumbing" 去发表无关感慨。D 是错误的问题类型，用 yes/no 加地点回答 "how"（怎么做）的提问，答非所问。

items.situation: offering help to someone who seems stressed
items.speaker: Can I take something off your plate?

options.A: The plates are in the top cabinet.
options.B: I already ate before I came here.
options.C: Where did you buy those dishes?
options.D: Honestly, that would be a lifesaver.

items.explanation: 正确答案 D 用自然的会话套语 "that would be a lifesaver" 接受对方分担压力的好意，正确理解了习语 "take something off your plate"（帮忙分担），符合 idiomatic 范式。A 是语义联想陷阱，把 "plate" 字面理解成盘子去谈橱柜位置。B 也是语义陷阱，把 "plate" 联想到吃饭去谈自己吃过了。C 同样是语义陷阱，抓住餐具相关的 "dishes" 去问购买地点，全都误读了习语含义。
